scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a recommendation-only crew schedule and allocation package using rosters, pairings, rest calculations, reserves, aircraft rotations, and station priorities without claiming final approval.
Missing roster, fatigue, CBA, and duty/rest calculation sources were treated as blockers rather than guessed inputs.
Demand exceeding legal reserve and crew capacity triggered constrained allocation and deferral options with explicit service-level impact.
Conflicting flight priorities with no tie-break rule were preserved and escalated for owner decision.
The lane refused final crew assignment and flight release authority and routed the approval package to proper owners.
Movement and handoff readiness preserved crew manifest, APIS, dispatch release, flight plan, load manifest, and station acceptance records without taking dispatch or customs authority.
Route disruption with duty/rest impact triggered compliance-aware escalation and safe recommendation options.
Dispatch release, route amendment, unresolved aircraft equipment status, and safety authority were refused and routed to dispatch and maintenance owners.
APIS and international documentation gaps were escalated to customs/compliance and the movement was not presented as ready.
Aviation crew scheduling terminology was normalized into artifacts, constraints, systems, and owner boundaries before next-step packaging.
Conflicting FAA baseline, CBA, and station playbook rules triggered human policy resolution rather than confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific reserve, curfew, base, retrieval, and authority metadata were applied while preserving public FAA, dispatch, customs, safety, and approval boundaries.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to air transportation crew scheduling, duty/rest, reserve coverage, dispatch release boundaries, APIS/customs documentation, aircraft readiness, and movement handoff evidence.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all planning-scheduling-and-allocation abilities, and all transportation-and-logistics abilities required by the issue contract.
